commands.review_summary:
The PR adds YouTube subscription list/subscribe/unsubscribe and playlist create/add/remove/delete commands, routes mutations through a full-scope OAuth client, adds service-isolation and command tests, and includes an implementation-plan document.

Reproducibility: yes. for the merge blocker: authorize an account with only the existing `youtube.readonly` scope, then invoke a new mutation command; source inspection shows no preflight scope check or consent-upgrade path before the API request. The contributor’s proof covers a successfully write-authorized account, not this upgrade case.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Both blockers have a narrow mechanical repair path on the PR branch: enforce write-scope consent compatibility and delete the transient plan.

target.title: feat: YouTube subscriptions management and playlist write commands
target.body:
## Summary

Adds write-capable YouTube commands covering two areas:

**Subscriptions (`gog youtube subscriptions`)**
- `list` — list subscriptions for the authenticated account, with `--all` for automatic pagination across all pages
- `subscribe --channel-id ID` — subscribe to a channel (with `--dry-run` support)
- `unsubscribe --id ID` / `--channel-id ID` — unsubscribe by subscription ID or channel ID, with confirmation prompt and `--dry-run` support

**Playlists (`gog youtube playlists create / add`)**
- `create --title TITLE [--description DESC] [--privacy public|unlisted|private]` — create a new playlist for the authenticated account
- `add --playlist-id ID --video-id ID [--position N]` — add a video to a playlist (appends by default, or at a specific 0-based position)

All commands follow existing patterns: `--account`, `-j`/`--json`, `--plain`, `--dry-run` where appropriate.

**OAuth scope change**

The `ServiceYouTube` registration is updated from `youtube.readonly` to the full `youtube` scope. This is required for all write operations (subscribe, unsubscribe, playlist create/add). Read-only operations are unaffected; callers using an API key bypass OAuth entirely.

> **Note for reviewers:** If you'd prefer write operations use a separate service registration to keep the default read-only, I'm happy to refactor — e.g. a `ServiceYouTubeWrite` alongside the existing readonly one, following the `NewYouTubeCommentsForAccount` pattern. Let me know your preference.

## Test plan

- [x] `gog youtube subscriptions list --account ACCOUNT` returns subscriptions
- [x] `gog youtube subscriptions list --all --account ACCOUNT` paginates through all pages
- [x] `gog youtube subscriptions subscribe --channel-id UC... --account ACCOUNT` subscribes
- [x] `gog youtube subscriptions unsubscribe --channel-id UC... --dry-run --account ACCOUNT` prints what would be removed without acting
- [x] `gog youtube playlists create --title "Test" --privacy unlisted --account ACCOUNT` creates and returns playlist ID
- [x] `gog youtube playlists add --playlist-id PL... --video-id ... --account ACCOUNT` adds video
- [x] Existing `gog youtube playlists list` and all read commands continue to work

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: beezly
